source_pr.title: lightning: revert custom gRPC resolver
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/33114

Problem Summary:
https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/32876 introduces a custom gRPC resolver to  tikv node address changes. However, it doesn't use tls config correctly. 


### What is changed and how it works?

Partial revert the custom gRPC part of https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/32876. 
Custom gRPC is not needed. See https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/32875#issuecomment-1068693681.

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [x] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- bugfix or new feature needs a release note -->

```release-note
None
```

root_cause_explanation: The Lightning/BR code introduced a custom gRPC resolver for TiKV address changes, but it did not handle TLS configuration correctly. This could cause environment/network-dependent failures in Lightning tests. This PR stabilizes the behavior by reverting the custom resolver path and relying on the standard gRPC resolver/TLS handling.
fix_pattern: Revert/remove custom network resolver logic that mishandles TLS or cluster address changes; rely on standard gRPC resolver/TLS handling to avoid environment-dependent failures.

evidence: files: br/pkg/lightning/backend/local/local.go, br/pkg/lightning/backend/local/localhelper_test.go, br/pkg/lightning/lightning.go, br/pkg/lightning/restore/restore.go, br/pkg/resolver/resolver.go, br/pkg/resolver/resolver_test.go, br/pkg/restore/split_client.go, br/pkg/restore/split_test.go, br/tests/br_split_region_fail/run.sh, br/tests/lightning_fail_fast/run.sh
